title: covillea divaricata
intro: Covillea divaricata, also known as Mexican silver mist or Mexican plum, is a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is a shrub or small tree that produces small, white flowers and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant's leaves and bark have been used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin issues, due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Covillea divaricata can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is also used in topical applications for wound healing and skin conditions.
